title: cryptocarya hartleyi
intro: Cryptocarya hartleyi, also known as the "Hartley's Cryptocarya" or "Mountain pepper," is a species of evergreen tree native to the mountains of Papua New Guinea and northern Australia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Cryptocarya hartleyi include relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as reducing fever and inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant include extracts and oils, which can be used in teas, capsules, and topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
